meta.title: DIAS3 — remote seizure risk after cerebral venous thrombosis
meta.cff_info:
DIAS3 Score

Lindgren et al., 2024

DOI: 10.1001/jamaneurol.2024.3481

Factors and Hazard Ratios (HR)

  • Decompressive hemicraniectomy: HR 2.28 (95% CI: 1.45-3.57), Coefficient: 0.823
  • Intracerebral hemorrhage (ICH) at baseline: HR 1.45 (95% CI: 1.11-1.87), Coefficient: 0.368
  • Age (per decade): HR 1.01 (95% CI: 0.94-1.09), Coefficient: 0.013; Age: 18–80 years (based on the 1st–99th percentile of the development dataset). For values outside this range, the calculator assigns risk at the nearest percentile and should be used with caution.
  • Seizures in acute phase (excluding status epilepticus): HR 1.52 (95% CI: 1.18-1.96), Coefficient: 0.420
  • Status epilepticus in acute phase: HR 2.35 (95% CI: 1.65-3.36), Coefficient: 0.856
  • Subdural hematoma (SDH) at baseline: HR 1.28 (95% CI: 0.74-2.21), Coefficient: 0.247

Formula for DIAS3 Score

Individual risk = 1 − (1 − baseline hazard)exp(A−0.4390308)
Where A = 0.823 (if decompressive hemicraniectomy) + 0.368 (if intracerebral hemorrhage) + 0.013 × age (per decade) + 0.420 (if acute seizure) + 0.856 (if status epilepticus) + 0.247 (if subdural hematoma).

Baseline Hazards:
- 1 year = 0.09996527
- 3 years = 0.14976

Risk Estimates

Risk at 1 and 3 years based on various scenarios:

Scenario 1-Year Risk 3-Year Risk
No ICH, No decompressive surgery, No SDH, No acute seizures ~7% ~10%
No ICH, No decompressive surgery, No SDH, Acute seizures excluding status epilepticus ~10% ~15%
No ICH, No decompressive surgery, No SDH, Status epilepticus in acute phase ~15% ~23%
No ICH, No decompressive surgery, No SDH, Acute seizures & status epilepticus ~22% ~32%
No ICH, Decompressive surgery, No SDH, No acute seizures ~15% ~22%
No ICH, Decompressive surgery, No SDH, Acute seizures excluding status epilepticus ~22% ~31%
No ICH, Decompressive surgery, No SDH, Status epilepticus ~31% ~44%
No ICH, Decompressive surgery, No SDH, Acute seizures & status epilepticus ~43% ~59%
ICH, No decompressive surgery, No SDH, No acute seizures ~10% ~15%
ICH, No decompressive surgery, No SDH, Acute seizures excluding status epilepticus ~14% ~21%
ICH, No decompressive surgery, No SDH, Status epilepticus ~21% ~31%
ICH, No decompressive surgery, No SDH, Acute seizures & status epilepticus ~30% ~43%
ICH, Decompressive surgery, No SDH, No acute seizures ~21% ~30%
ICH, Decompressive surgery, No SDH, Acute seizures excluding status epilepticus ~29% ~42%
ICH, Decompressive surgery, No SDH, Status epilepticus ~42% ~56%
ICH, Decompressive surgery, No SDH, Acute seizures & status epilepticus ~56% ~72%

Clinical Utility

The DIAS3 score provides a comprehensive, personalized risk prediction for epilepsy following cerebral venous thrombosis (CVT). It incorporates multiple clinical and imaging factors to guide clinical decision-making for long-term epilepsy management.

Additional Study Information

Category Details
Score Name DIAS3 Score
Population Derivation cohort: 1128 patients (International CVT Consortium)
Validation cohorts: 543 (ACTION-CVT) and 556 (Israel CVT study)
Statistical Methods Cox proportional hazards regression with multiple imputation and ridge penalty adjustments
Validation Methods Internal validation with bootstrapping
External validation using two independent multicenter cohorts
Performance Metrics C-statistics for 1- and 3-year follow-up:
- Derivation cohort: 0.74
- ACTION-CVT cohort: 0.76 (1-year), 0.77 (3-year)
- Israel CVT cohort: 0.80 (1- and 3-year)
Calibration plots indicated adequate agreement.
Generalizability High (validated internationally across multiple centers with diverse patient cohorts)
Clinical Utility Provides personalized epilepsy risk estimates after CVT for up to 3 years, guiding clinical decisions.
meta.authors: Lindgren E, Shu L, Simaan N, et al.
meta.clinical_utility: Importance One of 10 patients develop epilepsy in the late phase after cerebral venous thrombosis (CVT) diagnosis but predicting the individual risk is difficult. Objective To develop and externally validate a prognostic score to estimate the individual risk of post-CVT epilepsy.
meta.outcome: Development and Validation of a Clinical Score to Predict Epilepsy After Cerebral Venous Thrombosis

predictors.name: Decompressive Hemicraniectomy
predictors.assess: Surgical procedure involving removal of part of the skull to relieve intracranial pressure. Contributes a hazard coefficient of 0.823 to the DIAS3 score.

predictors.name: Intracerebral Hemorrhage (ICH)
predictors.assess: Bleeding within the brain parenchyma at baseline. Contributes a hazard coefficient of 0.368 to the DIAS3 score.

predictors.name: Acute Phase Seizures (excluding status epilepticus)
predictors.assess: Seizures occurring within the first seven days of cerebral venous thrombosis. Contributes a hazard coefficient of 0.420 to the DIAS3 score.

predictors.name: Status Epilepticus
predictors.assess: Seizures occurring within the first seven days of cerebral venous thrombosis. Contributes a hazard coefficient of 0.420 to the DIAS3 score.

predictors.name: Subdural Hematoma (SDH)
predictors.assess: Blood accumulation between the dura mater and arachnoid membrane at baseline. Contributes a hazard coefficient of 0.247 to the DIAS3 score.

predictors.name: Age
predictors.assess: Patient age divided by 10, entered as a whole number (e.g. 50 years = 5). Each decade adds a hazard coefficient of 0.013.

model.note: Logistic/complementary-log-log model reproduced from the DIAS3 regression coefficients. (The live-site chart is non-functional; this uses the published formula.)
model.viz_info: This is an additive risk model. The waterfall (left) begins at the baseline risk for a patient with no risk factors, then each factor you select steps the risk up (blue) or down (red) — the number on each bar is that factor's contribution in percentage points, so you can see which factors dominate. The donut (right) shows the same active factors as coloured wedges that sum to the total predicted risk shown in the centre. Both update live as you change the inputs.
